title: Sehima nervosa

intro: Sehima nervosa, also known as the "nervous grass," is a plant species belonging to the family Poaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, due to its potential sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sehima nervosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Sehima nervosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures to promote relaxation and alleviate stress.
